subject: Project Management
title: Kahneman and Tversky's Discovery
description: A quick win: understand Kahneman and Tversky's Discovery.

hook.text: Daniel Kahneman's own textbook project was estimated at 2 years. It took 8. He studied why.

buildup.text: Kahneman and Amos Tversky found that people consistently underestimate time, cost, and risk.

discovery.text: They named it the 'planning fallacy' — our systematic tendency to be too optimistic about our plans.

twist.text: Even knowing the fallacy exists doesn't protect you. Kahneman himself fell for it — while studying it.

climax.text: The planning fallacy became one of the most cited concepts in behavioral economics and project management.

punchline.text: The man who named the trap still fell into it.

quiz.question: What happened with Kahneman's own textbook project?
options: It was estimated at 2 years but took 8 years
It was completed ahead of schedule
He abandoned it after discovering the fallacy
